steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: A margin of error applied to a sample mean produces a confidence interval. With mean $23.4$ and margin of error $1.3$: lower bound $= 23.4 - 1.3 = 22.1$, upper bound $= 23.4 + 1.3 = 24.7$. The interval is $[22.1, 24.7]$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: A confidence interval conveys that the true population mean is plausible within the computed range, not that it is guaranteed to lie there. Values outside the interval are not impossible — they are simply less supported by this sample.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The interval describes the mean of the population, not the range of every individual data value in the population. Therefore, Choice A is correct.
